programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Shop: H2O e3 Cleaning System
programme.desc: Clean, sanitise, and help deodorise your home using just simple kitchen ingredients and electricity. The H2O e3 supercharges water with the salt you eat to create a multi purpose super cleaner!

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac, Stella and Lindsay discover a link between the deaths of a tram driver and a dancer who recently won the lottery. Meanwhile, Danny and Hawkes investigate the murder of a fish merchant and are led to a jealous friend who is resentful that his child was refused entry to a prestigious school

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac, Danny, Flack and Lindsay investigate the case of a middle-aged tycoon killed in a lift, and discover a world in which girls meet much older men for sexual encounters. Stella and Hawkes uncover the identity of a body found in Central Park, learning the victim had been living a double life which ultimately led to his death. Drama, starring Gary Sinise

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Miami detective Horatio Caine enlists Mac's help as he pursues serial killer Henry Darius in New York. However, he soon loses the trail and is forced to follow other leads. An investigation into the sister of one of Darius's victims proves a dead end, but the team soon finds a vital link between the murderer and one of his hostages. David Caruso guest stars

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Return of the military drama. The team is reunited when a nuclear-powered satellite is knocked out of orbit. Meanwhile, Paige and Walter confront their feelings

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A woman from Cabe's past asks for help in apprehending a Serbian war criminal, so the team travels to Cuba to track him down. Walter faces a judge following his car accident

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is stabbed to death after stepping out of her shower, becoming the latest victim of the Dead Rose Slasher. Gibbs is approached by his old friend and barber Frankie Dean, who shares his concern that his son Cameron may be the killer. He explains that a photo-fit released to the media resembles him, and the attacks stopped during a period coinciding with his three-year stint in the navy. Meanwhile, Tony has to take probationary agent Ned Dorneget under his wing

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Marine sergeant Teddy Lemere is killed by a sniper in Afghanistan just after his sniffer dog helps guide a young child out of a minefield. Back in the US, his widow Ruby tells Gibbs and the team that she had recently received a message from her husband saying he might have upset a few people. Abby retrieves a tiny shard of the sniper's bullet from Ted's helmet and finds that it was made in America

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A doctor dies on board the USS Borealis during a heavy storm and when the ship is brought into Norfolk Naval Yard, Ducky's investigation reveals a telltale mark on the victim's carotid artery, suggesting he was injected with poison. Initial inquiries focus on a petty officer with a history of drug use who may have wanted the doctor to remain silent about his problems. American drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team are called in when a Navy reservist returns home to find her husband missing and her living room covered in blood, and make a grim discovery when they find a finger in the bin. Meanwhile, Ziva ruffles a few feathers by insisting on continuing her investigation into the murder of her father, asking for permission to go to Rome on the trail of the chief suspect. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: New Mossad director Orli Elbaz asks for the team's help in finding Ilan Bodnar when one of her agents is shot dead in Virginia, telling Gibbs he was investigating several operatives who had gone rogue after Eli David's death. Ziva travels with Tony to Berlin in her pursuit of Bodnar, the man she believes was responsible for the death of her father. Guest starring Marina Sirtis (Star Trek: The Next Generation)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Return of the military drama. The team is reunited when a nuclear-powered satellite is knocked out of orbit. Meanwhile, Paige and Walter confront their feelings

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A woman from Cabe's past asks for help in apprehending a Serbian war criminal, so the team travels to Cuba to track him down. Walter faces a judge following his car accident

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the Secretary of the Navy, learns her daughter has been kidnapped, and the NCIS crew teams up with the FBI to track the case, determine a motive and bring the abducted girl home. Meanwhile, McGee's childhood friend, NCIS Special Agent Valerie Page, arrives in town to assist with advanced tactics training. Naval crime drama, guest starring Leslie Hope and Christina Chang

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team looks into a raid on a naval base, and Dr Cyril Taft - the surgeon who saved Gibbs' life - experiences the thrill of assisting on an investigation. Assigned to treat Gibbs' suspect, Taft manages to uncover evidence that may prove crucial to solving the case. Meanwhile, DiNozzo pays his old flame Jeanne Benoit a visit as he chases a lead involving doctors in Sudan. Guest starring John Cryer and Scottie Thompson

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A day spent working on a cut-and-dried case leaves the team free to spend some time away from work, and while McGee and Delilah argue over the importance of keeping shop-talk to a minimum, DiNozzo ends up boring his dinner date with crime-solving tales. However, the agents' personal plans are all interrupted as, one-by-one, they realise errors have been made in closing their latest investigation. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An American couple are attacked in Iraq, in the very same spot a group of US Special Ops snipers were ambushed six months earlier. Determined to get to the bottom of the case, Gibbs is forced to speak to Aaron Davis, the only survivor of the first assault, who is now struggling through PTSD at the Walter Reed Medical Centre. The 300th episode of the military crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy Public Affairs Officer is killed, and when the team takes up the case, both the man's wife and his mistress emerge as potential suspects. Each woman offering a very different take on the chain of events that led to the murder, and the investigators are forced to decide which of the two is telling the truth. Meanwhile, DiNozzo's father comes to the aid of a homeless woman, who makes a surprising claim about his relationship with her. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: A skilled marksman is arrested on an attempted murder charge of a superior officer, but escapes and takes refuge in the mountains. Harm is among the search party sent to find him, and faces danger as events unfold

programme.desc: Calleigh's car is run off the road and into a canal while she is en route back to the lab - and although she is unhurt, vital evidence is compromised, jeopardising an investigation into a rogue biker gang. programme.desc: The team tracks Bodnar's SUV to a remote house in the countryside, but inside find only his dead accomplice - identified as a South African mercenary who helped Bodnar procure diamonds. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Neil is badly let down by one of his regular informants when a family jewellers is robbed of half a million pounds' worth of stock and one of the partners is shot in the course of the crime. Phil plays the knight in shining armour for casino owner's wife Monica Dreyfuss when she turns up in custody drunk and unable to pay her restaurant bill

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A worried mother reports her son missing after a night out clubbing, but finds it difficult to accept he is gay when it emerges he has been beaten and raped following an assignation with a casual pick-up. Emma and Sally have some success persuading the troubled youngster to unburden himself and Neil jumps to the conclusion the lad's own friends may have had something to do with the attack

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry investigates a loan shark accused of using strong-arm tactics to collect the extortionate repayments she demands. Stories about her employing a vicious thug to make acid attacks on the families of debtors prove all too true and Beth finds herself in danger when the ruffian is cornered. Meanwhile, a Ukrainian woman with no passport or visa is taken to hospital following a road accident but runs away before Sally can get the full story

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy picks up a lead on a drug dealer operating from a burger van and before long Sun Hill's uniform branch are mounting a raid on a skunk factory - where Diane puts herself in great danger by ignoring Callum's orders. Back at the station, Phil and Stuart muster as much empathy and sensitivity as they can when CID are called on to sort out a love triangle and find a missing bridegroom in time for his wedding

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Charity Wakefield and Hattie Morahan lead the cast in Andrew Davies's adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel about a voyage of sexual and romantic discovery. Following the death of her husband, Mrs Dashwood and her daughters are forced to give up their home and move to a small cottage in Devonshire. Elinor is disappointed to leave her old home after falling in love with Edward Ferrars, her sister-in-law's brother, but looks forward to a promised visit. Marianne quickly finds she has an admirer in Colonel Brandon but is swept of her feet by neighbour Willoughby

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Colonel Brandon is called away on urgent business, leaving Marianne free to fall blissfully in love with Willoughby - until he too suddenly leaves for London. Meanwhile, Elinor has a disappointing meeting with Edward, but the reasons for his distant behaviour become clear when Sir John's niece Lucy Steele entrusts her with a shocking secret

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Marianne's health deteriorates as Colonel Brandon reveals the full extent of Willoughby's betrayal. Meanwhile, Edward states his intentions to honour his engagement to Lucy Steele, despite being disinherited by his mother as a result. Charity Wakefield and Dominic Cooper star

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Secret
programme.desc: Feature-length dramatisation of the writer's only thriller, set in 19th-century Tyneside. Colin Buchanan stars as successful businessman Freddie Musgrave, who has worked hard for 20 years to live down his misspent youth as runner to a gang of smugglers and is shocked when a face appears from long ago, threatening to reveal the details of his sordid past. With Clare Higgins and June Whitfield

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Cynthia is concerned during a visit as she feels new mum Pamela is overly anxious, so Dr Turner arranges for a psychiatrist to come in. However, they soon discover both mother and baby have gone missing. Meanwhile, Jenny, on secondment, begins to see cracks in the establishment, Chummy discovers her mother has walked out on her father, and Trixie struggles to hide her disappointment when Tom cancels a date. Guest starring Sophie Rundle (The Bletchley Circle)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Jenny is assigned to first-time mother Jeanette, who requires frequent visits during a protracted labour, and when she meets her patient's cousin, Philip, a new friendship begins to grow for the midwife. Meanwhile, Chummy worries about her mother's illness when she discovers Lady Browne has discharged herself from hospital. Jessica Raine and Miranda Hart star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Treasure hunters arrive on the island to track down the loot of a legendary pirate, a search that comes to an abrupt halt when the party's geologist is shot dead in leader Daniel Morgan's tent. As the murder weapon was a 300-year-old pistol, DI Poole decides every member of the team is a suspect - but the investigation takes a strange turn when Daniel falls victim to a series of unfortunate and coincidental accidents. Has a mythical pirate's curse become a reality or is someone from the 21st century simply trying to bump him off? Ben Miller and Sara Martins star, with Michael Brandon (Dempsey and Makepeace) and Kelly Adams (Hustle)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki and the Lyell team investigate a suspicious death from 20 years previously after a skeleton is found in a concrete pillar in a multi-storey car park.

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: It looks as if ambitious politician Malcolm Innes's career has reached its zenith as he prepares to clinch a multimillion-pound investment deal that will bring thousands of jobs to Glasgow. However, the presence of angry protesters threatens to derail the project - and Innes' troubles are compounded by the fact that his own son is among their number. Cop drama, starring James Macpherson and Blythe Duff

programme.title: Gregory's Girl (1981)
programme.desc: A gawky teenager is dismayed to be relegated to goalkeeper in the school football team - but his thoughts turn to romance once he meets the female striker who takes his place. However, his awkward nature proves an obstacle to winning her over, despite the sage advice of his worldly-wise little sister. Comedy, starring John Gordon Sinclair, Dee Hepburn, Jake D'Arcy, Clare Grogan and Robert Buchanan

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective and Havers are sent to the Scottish Highlands to investigate the murder of a respected playwright, only to be confronted by glamorous stars from the film industry. As events unfold, they discover sexual jealousy, unstable relationships and numerous lies behind the glossy showbiz facade. Detective drama, guest starring James McAvoy, with Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An unknown tagger begins to paint graffiti around London, and claims to have been responsible for killing a celebrated street artist four years previously. Originally part of a gang, the victim went solo after receiving support from a wealthy art patron, and when the detectives reopen the case, they become suspicious of his resentful former crew members. Meanwhile, Brian signs up to a social networking site as TopCop999 to chart the gritty life of a lawman, much to his colleagues' displeasure

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The appeal of a man convicted of murder and mutilation forces Grace to question the decision she made on the case 30 years ago. Someone who appears to be copying his chilling tactics sends the team footage of a new victim having his finger cut off. At the same time, Grace receives threats and a macabre package - a jar of pickled, severed fingers. Sue Johnston and Trevor Eve star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Phil's latest conquest Monica Dreyfuss reports a burglary at her home which has left her bruised, shaken and fearing for her life. A series of beatings on a local estate points to a vigilante campaign against trouble-making drug dealers and the case touches a nerve with Will as he struggles to get over Martin Parks' suicide. Michael Brandon and Anna Wing guest star

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: With three items saved from an Altrincham skip, Sarah Moore spruces up a table, Guy Trench helps an old vice find a new home, and Daniel Heath put his twist on a pair of chairs

programme.title: The NeverEnding Story (1984)
programme.desc: A lonely boy, neglected by his father and bullied at school, finds comfort in a mysterious book about a young hero's quest to save his world from destruction. However, as he reads the tale, he begins to become part of the story. Fantasy adventure based on Michael Ende's novel, starring Barret Oliver, Noah Hathaway, Gerald McRaney, Moses Gunn and Patricia Hayes

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how Earth's five oceans connect to form the largest ecosystem on the planet, and how its network of currents supports the health of the seas and marine wildlife. Cameras follow vast super-pods of dolphins and the flocks of gannets that follow them as they scour the oceans searching for food, as well as the tiny yet dazzling flamboyant cuttlefish that's looking for a mate

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison discovers how communities on her home turf of Gloucestershire are pulling together to one another during the pandemic. She meets the producers on a mission to get everyone eating local and healthy meals at affordable prices, finds out why gardening is on prescription, and helps out with the endangered hedgehog, which is facing a precarious future. Wildlife film-maker Jack Perks gets up close to dippers, Adam Henson sizes up a new boar for his pigs and Charlotte Smith investigates what future trade deals could mean for the food on the nation's plates

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Newby Hall & Gardens in Ripon, North Yorkshire. Treasures include an intriguing letter from Lord Nelson, politely replying to his optician just weeks before the Battle of Trafalgar. There's also a piece of the set from Live Aid in 1985, rescued from a skip, and an engagement ring picked up in a pawn shop. The Bradford Museum also launches an appeal to find the families related to thousands of photos in their archive

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at the effect human activity is having, revealing how the carbon dioxide being produced is destabilising life on Earth. There is footage of rescued orphaned elephants in Kenya, victims of ever-worsening droughts, while ocean patrols off the coast of Gabon fight to save endangered sharks. In the Amazon, wildlife teams are saving animals in the shrinking rainforest, and in San Diego a zoo is freezing the DNA of endangered species before they go extinct

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Herman's behaviour becomes increasingly erratic and he is sent on compulsory leave. Will a private inspection of Kanit House bring him, Angela and their colleagues the major breakthrough they crave? Charles, meanwhile, is in Karachi attempting to set up business in Europe and as he attempts to snare a buyer for his gems, he faces up to memories of his past, while Marie-Andree and Ajay vie for ascendancy within the group

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring West Ham United v Liverpool at London Stadium, Leicester City v Leeds United at King Power Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v Tottenham Hotspur at Amex Stadium. Liverpool, Leicester and Spurs will all hold aspirations of winning the league in what promises to be a closely fought and unpredictable title-race. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur, Everton v Manchester United and Aston Villa v Arsenal

programme.title: The Bling Ring (2013)
programme.desc: Five teenagers united by an obsession with celebrity steal from the houses of the rich and famous stars they idolise, using the internet to follow their targets' movements. When the police finally catch up with them, the arrest brings a taste of the fame they have craved. Fact-based drama, starring Emma Watson and Israel Broussard

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the progress of a house in Brierley Hill in the West Midlands, a former bank in Hyde, Greater Manchester, and a terraced home in Stanley Grove in London

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: Chris Bavin helps families sample rural living to see if it could bring them a new lease of life. A couple plan to move from Cheshire to the Dorset coast

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A trap is set to catch a pair of bogus builders, a thief regrets leaving his own bike behind at the scene of his crime, and a burglar is too drunk to make his escape

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, where Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper are helping today's reds and blues. Natasha also talks to Ben about his family

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of opera singer José Castana, who seemed to have no enemies. Return of the crime drama, starring Elen Rhys and Julian Looman

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The duo go baking in Italy, where dishes include a classic focaccia with rosemary, and pasta pie. They also pay a visit to Si's sister, who lives in Tuscany

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple wanting to uproot from Essex and find a modest cottage in Cornwall to call their forever home. The two nurses have a budget of £340,000

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The programme meets Melissa and Kenny, who breed and train sheepdogs, while Billy and his nephew Jock are hoping for success at the sheepdog trials

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other for items brought in by the public, including navigation lights, a watch repair kit, a vintage corkscrew, a mirror and two plaster heads

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Labour Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help the Rev Karen and husband Colin, who have £6,000 to turn their oddly-shaped and weed-filled garden in Romsey into an Italian retreat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon panics when Linda mentions Max is getting close to working out who attacked Ian. Chelsea is more determined than ever to make things work, but Denise and Patrick become suspicious of what she is up to. An upset Billy asks Phil for work, but when his cousin sides with Jay, Billy's anger gets the better of him and he tells the kids about Honey

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman, the Rev Kate Bottley, Sara Barron and Ed Gamble join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds such as Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: After a leading trainer shot and badly wounded, a former boxer executed in broad daylight, and a gangland killing at a weigh-in for a European title fight, there is evidence that organised crime is involved in professional boxing. Darragh MacIntyre investigates the murky world behind the glamour of the big fights and the suspected gangster who has been welcomed to boxing's top table. Daniel Kinahan has been named in the courts as the head of one of Europe's biggest drug cartels. So how did he become involved in setting up the fight between Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua to become the undisputed heavyweight champion of the world?

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The sewing skills of the contestants are tested as they are pitted against each other in a design challenge called Who Wore it Best? Supermodel Jourdan Dunn joins Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood star Sam Neill, who is appearing in new Australian comedy-drama Rams, and actress Billie Piper, who talks about her film Rare Beasts, which she wrote, directed and stars in. Cold Feet star James Nesbitt discusses his new Northern Ireland-set BBC series Bloodlands, while comedian Ando Osho promotes her debut novel Asking for a Friend. Plus, music legend Tom Jones chats and performs his new single No Hole in My Head

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of a former pub in Sittingbourne in Kent, a mid-terrace in Creswell, Nottinghamshire and a terraced house in Fulham, west London

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: Andreena and partner Germaine feel they have outgrown their home in Walthamstow. They want to sample a simpler life in Kent, but have to convince their daughter Renee to join them

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Detectives trail a burglar who is amongst the most callous they have ever encountered, and a trusted employee runs out of luck when she is caught stealing scratch cards

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and David Harper help two teams scour Nottingham racecourse for items to take to auction, while Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about one of the city's great authors

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Max and Miranda investigate a missing person case, during which they dig up a series of historical murders linking a mass grave to a washed-up private eye. Phil Daniels guest stars

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The penultimate leg of Si King and Dave Myers' European baking tour takes them to France. Travelling from Lyon to Carcassonne, they learn the secrets of teeth-pulling nougat, go truffle hunting and meet up with Dave's old flatmate, whom he has not seen for 30 years. Culinary delights en route include tarte aux pralines, a savoury-stuffed brioche, apricot tarte Tatin and, of course, the traditional French baguette

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from Northampton find a new home in Norfolk with spectacular views. He also helps a local chef to prepare some seasonal shellfish caught on the coast

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The return of the programme celebrating inspirational farming families and the rural events where they showcase their hard work. Poultry farmers Nick and Jacob and organic and heritage fruit and vegetable farmer Patrick display their wares at Alexandra Palace Farmers' Market

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other for items brought in by the public including a set of soda syphons, a collection of football figures and a curious and shocking machine

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda's suspicions grow after Max's shocking announcement and she is determined to get the truth. Denise accuses Lucas of manipulating Chelsea. Honey suggests talking to the kids about her and Jay, but is angry when Billy, still sulking, shows up late

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The Darwin team struggles to save patients as the hospital goes into lockdown. Cameron faces being exposed as completely out of his depth, and is forced to phone Nicky for advice in theatre. Alex tries to help a stressed Donna to remain calm, stirring up some unresolved feelings, while Dom decides to return to the world of internet dating

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Edinburgh, ambulance crew Stephen and Gwion are called to a festival worker who needs treatment for a reaction to a bee sting. An elderly patient with labyrinthitis has become dizzy and needs to be taken to hospital. A sepsis case at a GP surgery needs emergency treatment and a fast transfer to hospital.

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry challenges the celebrity cooks to prepare the perfect breakfast for houseguests. Two of the contestants adopt a Mexican theme, pitting quesadillas against breakfast burritos, while another presents the judges with an edible crossword. Mary and fellow judges Angela Hartnett and Chris Bavin each select their favourite dish, but even this is no protection against being eliminated from the contest. Claudia Winkleman presents

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Manchester United v Southampton, Sheffield United v West Bromwich Albion, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Arsenal and Newcastle United v Crystal Palace

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic 4x100m gold medallist Mark Lewis-Francis, swimming great Rebecca Adlington and Scottish internationals in football and rugby union respectively Charlie Adam and John Barclay join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Gill Johnson was convinced Australia would be the perfect place for her and her family to settle down. Husband Mark, however, was less than enthusiastic. Would a week in Perth persuade him that she wasn't just on a flight of fancy? Three years since their trial week, Nicki Chapman catches up with the pair to see whether they made the move Gill so desperately wanted

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Christine Walkden explores the gardens of England's northernmost county Northumberland, and participates in tree-planting at Cragside, where she meets 'fern queen' Jennifer. The horticulturist also visits Holy Island and hears how over-exuberant Edwardian planting is undermining the castle foundations. Plus, the story of engineer William Armstrong

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Winter returns and an abandoned Mozart tries to look after her newborn pups alone. Meanwhile, the rival Commandos force a confrontation with the Whiskers. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower leads the Whiskers to better hunting grounds. However, the gang strays into the territory of the rival Zappa clan, and the ensuing battle leaves them searching for a missing pup

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower sets out to rescue the pups from the dangers of Zappa territory, but it is up to Mitch to complete the mission. Meanwhile, Mozart and Carlos make an appearance, now the leaders of their own groups. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker is staying close to home exploring the Chilterns landscape and the people and skills that have shaped this Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. He discovers some hidden historical gems revealed by a revolutionary new mapping technique, heads deep into the woods to learn about the ancient art of bodging and rolls his sleeves up to help clean up ponds which are so desperately needed by wildlife. Joe Crowley investigates if large- scale schemes such as HS2 tally with the Government's pledge to go green, while Adam Henson calls in a horse whisperer to help with a nervous Exmoor pony

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: On the wild north Atlantic coast, Rick heads out to sea to go fishing for lobsters with his old friend and celebrity chef Nathan Outlaw, before helping to cook up a lobster risotto. Later, at Tresillian House near Newquay, Rick meets with head gardener John Harris to find out more about his Victorian walled garden

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The remaining nine talented celebrity home cooks are put through their paces as they face a series of challenges. First, Mary Berry tasks the famous faces to make a chocolate dessert fit for a romantic date night, before the competitors take on Chris Bavin's tricky challenge of rustling up a dish using his favourite cut of leftover meat. The judges must then decide, taking both challenges into account, who is safe and who needs to prove themselves by cooking again in Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator

programme.title: Le Mans (1971)
programme.desc: A racing driver battles against the odds to recover from a serious injury, determined to take on his arch rival in the prestigious 24-hour competition - and win a widow's heart. Drama, starring Steve McQueen, Elga Andersen, Ronald Leigh-Hunt and Christopher Waite

programme.title: The Train (1964)
programme.desc: As the Allies look set to liberate Paris from the Nazi forces, the Germans resolve to take all they can from the city - and plot to transport national treasures back to Berlin. However, bold French Resistance forces vow to stop them, enlisting the help of a train inspector along the way. Second World War adventure, starring Burt Lancaster, Paul Scofield, Jeanne Moreau and Michel Simon

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Action from the night slalom from Schladming in Austria, which did not have the usual 50,000 fans present, but still featured the best technical skiers in the world. Plus, highlights of the women's downhill from the treacherous Kandahar course in Garmisch-Partenkirchen, Germany

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The documentary follows the Toms as they try to resurrect the last boat built in Looe and invest in the future of the town's fishing fleet, with three generations throwing themselves into the fray. The Toms' journey to get the boat back out and fishing takes them through the challenges of Britain's quota system, in which just five families own nearly a third of the country's entire fish quota

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The globetrotting presenter recalls some of the most dangerous experiences of his travels, from coming under fire in war-torn Mogadishu to squaring off with a female wrestler in Mexico City. Among the past destinations featured are a police cell in the far east of Russia, and the programme also looks back at his encounters with ambulance crews in San Salvador and anti-smuggling police in Italy

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh heads to more countries that are rarely visited by tourists to get a sense of the local culture and find out if they deserve their reputation. In this first edition, he heads to Zimbabwe in the aftermath of Robert Mugabe's rule, to discover whether British people should be holidaying in the southern African country once again. He meets Chipo, a teacher who was born and raised in Harare under Mugabe's rule. She explains that although people have been coming to the country, few venture beyond Victoria Falls. She wants to show Rom what they are missing on a grand, epic road trip that takes in National Parks and close-up encounters with rhinos, elephants and the so-called 'cheetah ambassador'

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert: Stand Up to Infertility
programme.desc: With he and his wife struggling to conceive, the comedian investigates male infertility, setting out to discover why nobody seems to be addressing the issue despite it accounting for around 50 per cent of all fertility cases. He discovers that the fact men don't want to talk about infertility is doing just as much harm as the condition itself

programme.title: Lance
programme.desc: Part two of two. A personal examination of the rise and fall of Lance Armstrong. The series examines the young Texan cyclist's rise to fame, his struggle with testicular cancer and subsequent emergence as a global icon with seven consecutive Tour de France titles, and his eventual downfall when he was revealed as being at the centre of one of the biggest doping scandals in sporting history

programme.title: The Homesman (2014)
programme.desc: A single woman living in a frontier town is asked by her church to escort three women traumatised by the hardships of their lives to a safe refuge in the East. Along the way, she saves an outlaw's life and persuades him to help her protect her charges on the perilous journey through the wilderness. Western based on the novel by Glendon Swarthout, directed by and starring Tommy Lee Jones, with Hilary Swank, Meryl Streep and Miranda Otto

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Matt uses his powerful form of interrogation to extract the truth from hostage Noah, who reveals how he became involved with Nathan's government plot to capture all the heroes. The mind-reader's discoveries then bring Peter face to face with the Hunter. Fantasy drama, guest starring Zeljko Ivanek, with Greg Grunberg, Jack Coleman and Milo Ventimiglia

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Using a tip-off from Rebel, Matt and Peter head to Building 26 to rescue Daphne and recover files that will expose the government's nefarious plans. Meanwhile, Claire finds help from an unlikely source in smuggling Alex out of the city, Sylar is led to the truth about his mother and the Hunter puts his explosive plot into action. Greg Grunberg, Milo Ventimiglia and Hayden Panettiere star

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac must fight for control of the hospital when a returning patient wreaks havoc. Reeling from Skylar's tragedy, the F1s try to cover their tracks when a flirtatious patient makes a serious accusation. Grieving Sacha is scared of letting go of his daughter while a desperate Jodie is on the loose, and his usual arsenal of lies, delays and deceptions may not be enough to keep Beka safe

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two Scottish farming families hope to make healthy sales at the Bowhouse market, Fife. To try to squeeze more income from their milk, husband and wife Robert and Jane, along with their son Ben now make 27 tonnes of award-winning cheese each year on their 660-acre dairy farm. Meanwhile, the Bruces gave up careers to start a small farm growing botanicals to make into spirits and liquors. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: Penguins: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Documentary following penguins living in unexpected environments, including satellite imagery of previously undiscovered colonies of flightless birds. Among their habitats are dense forests, desert burrows and city streets. The programme reveals the challenges they face in making their homes in these challenging environments, from evading predators to scaling rocks, and captures the lifelong bonds formed between family groups

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy.

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright embarks on a six-week mission to encourage 11- to 16-year-olds to get active, beginning with exercises designed to get muscles moving

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Topics include positive and negative numbers and forms of writing

programme.title: Black and British: A Forgotten History
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga explores the enduring relationship between Britain and people whose origins lie in Africa, looking back over significant people and events throughout history. He visits Cumbria, Eastbourne and Hereford to uncover the stories of generations of people with African descent in Britain, unearthing moments in history that have almost been forgotten. In Eastbourne, David comes face to face with the remains of an ancient, mysterious skeleton nicknamed Beachy Head Woman, with analysis revealing that she was of African descent yet raised in East Sussex

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Appeal on behalf of Ovarian Cancer Action, a charity campaigning to increase awareness and fund scientific research into the disease

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Paul Hayes and John Cameron visit an auction in Warrington and go head to head over a cameo brooch, and Paul resorts to sneaky tactics to hide items from his rival

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates this series exploring extreme behaviour in the natural world, uncovering some of the strategies animals have developed to stay alive and to breed, from Komodo dragons tackling buffalo to the courtship battle of the humpback whale. He begins by focusing on the methods used by creatures such as cheetahs, bottle-nosed dolphins and Venus fly-traps in their fights for survival

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers settle into new territory, but their neighbours are not friendly and the burrow is in danger. Meanwhile, Mozart and Kinkajou compete for the role of dominant female in the Starsky group. Bill Nighy narrates

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Interesting finds from the show's travels around the country, including a late-19th-century Steiff cow on rollers and a joystick from a World War One aeroplane

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Raj Bisram, Josie Lawrence, Mark Watson and Laura Whitmore test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: After a positive first couple of weeks, the temptation of unhealthy treats and alcohol is threatening to undo the group's progress, so Tom shares a series of recipes to help the volunteers with their temptations for unhealthy treats. He starts with his own take on Asian crispy duck pancakes, before demonstrating how to make fudge chocolate brownies which are lower in calories. Later, Tom visits Lauren in her home, where he gives her a lesson in making prawn and avocado rice paper rolls which contain lots of fresh, healthy ingredients

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys presents another heat of the classic quiz, where contenders take the famous black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock. Tonight's specialist subjects are Mammals of the British Isles, Friends, Damien Hirst and the Wimbledon Men's Singles Championship 1968 - Present

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as the Polyhymnians take on the Severns in a last chance to stay in the tournament. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The quarter-finals continue, with two more teams doing battle to reach the next stage in the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Padstow is well known for its tourist industry, but there's another lucrative industry here that few visitors are aware of. Each year, Padstow's fishermen land hundreds of tonnes of brown crab. But the vast majority bypass the town altogether. For decades, lorryloads of shellfish have left Padstow for the continent every week. Once connected to Europe, Padstow's crabbing industry boomed. Britain's crab exports to Europe form an industry worth over sixty million pounds. But lockdowns across Europe have brought everything to a standstill, and as the global market for crab grows, concerns are starting to be raised about the effects of overfishing

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actress and comedian Jo Brand hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, as stand-up Zoe Lyons and newspaper columnist Fintan O'Toole join regular captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part investigation into the initial Covid-19 outbreak in Wuhan, examining the gulf between what the authorities knew and what was made available to the public. Over the course of 54 days, Chinese officials maintained that the outbreak was under control, with only a few cases linked to a live animal market. However, the infection soon proved to be far more widespread, necessitating stricter lockdown measures

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker is staying close to home exploring the Chilterns landscape and the people and skills that have shaped this Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. He discovers some hidden historical gems revealed by a revolutionary new mapping technique, heads deep into the woods to learn about the ancient art of bodging and rolls his sleeves up to help clean up ponds which are so desperately needed by wildlife. Joe Crowley investigates if large- scale schemes such as HS2 tally with the Government's pledge to go green, while Adam Henson calls in a horse whisperer to help with a nervous Exmoor pony

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Winter wildlife stories from around the UK. Chris Packham will be in the New Forest watching winter transform his home, and Iolo Williams is at the Centre for Alternative Technology in Wales, hoping to see some murmurations. Young naturalist Indy Green will be taking us deep into Sherwood Forest on the hunt for one of our most elusive birds of prey - the goshawk

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a distinctive deer stalker hat was the red flag to cracking a web of stolen bank cards. Plus, how one careless text message put a dangerous stalker behind bars for the longest sentence in a police force's history. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: Chris Bavin helps families sample rural living to see if it could bring them a new lease of life. A couple plan to move from Cheshire to the Dorset coast

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The programme meets Melissa and Kenny, who breed and train sheepdogs, while Billy and his nephew Jock are hoping for success at the sheepdog trials

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The return of the documentary about the county's fishing communities, beginning in the popular tourist resort of Newquay as restrictions begin to ease after the coronavirus lockdown. It is not a moment too soon for the town's fishermen, most of whom have been tied up for months, and with lost time to catch up on, and livelihoods on the line, the fleet is steaming into a summer like no other

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright will bring fitness to secondary kids as he leads a daily dose of workout sessions, joined by a host of his celebrity friends

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Daily show to support home learning. Topics include grammar and expanding your vocabulary

programme.title: Black and British: A Forgotten History
programme.desc: Continuing his exploration of black British history, historian David Olusoga investigates further into the stories of thousands of Africans transported into slavery by the British, looking back at key historical events that turned Britain into the biggest slave-trading nation in the world, and shaped the future of Britain for black people. David learns about conflicting English laws that were implemented against the slaves and discovers more about the people who challenged these laws and contributed to the end of slavery

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Paul Hayes and John Cameron search for bargains at Ardingly Antiques Fair, where John hopes to get lucky with a wishing well and a painting takes Paul on a Shakespearean journey

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how reptiles and amphibians, including the pebble toad and Jesus Christ lizard, conquer their shortcomings by using tricks and strategies to avoid predators. He also explores how chameleons can achieve a high level of accuracy with their muscle-propelled, extendible tongues, and finds out how a 9ft-long Komodo dragon can bring down a huge water buffalo

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Womanising male Houdini is on the prowl - and female Flower is ready for breeding. But it remains to be seen whether the two hit it off. Pregnant Kinkajoo emerges as the group's dominant female and Mozart helps Carlos when he begins to suffer from an infected wound. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Ragley Hall, Warwickshire, antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Charlie Ross find a selection of antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Christina gets to grips with a weighty jardiniere, Charlie tests out a horse-measuring stick on its owner and presenter Paul Martin steps into the world of folk art

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Raj Bisram, Josie Lawrence, Mark Watson and Laura Whitmore go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef takes a culinary tour of France to discover what Britain's neighbours have to teach us. He begins his trip in Dieppe, where he prepares freshly caught turbot, forages for sea herbs along the Bay of the Somme, and cooks a seafood gratin. He then heads for southern Champagne, a region that was a favourite of Renoir

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Alan Carr hosts a new series of the interior design challenge, in which 10 aspiring designers compete to redesign Oxfordshire show home. Michelle Ogundehin assesses their efforts, and is joined in the first episode by guest judge Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: The second part of this two-part documentary explores the initial outbreak of Covid-19 in the US. When Covid-19 first arrived on American soil, US federal health authorities were seemingly unaware of how transmissible the virus truly was. This account chronicles 54 days in which the United States made crucial decisions that would severely impact the spread of the virus. Featuring interviews with public health officials and scientists who had already concluded that the virus had global pandemic potential, who were confronted with a failing diagnostic test and a president who appeared to repeatedly undermine the true threat of the virus

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of rest and recreation this week, with Stephen K Amos, Susan Calman, Lou Sanders and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: DeLorean: Back from the Future
programme.desc: Documentary charting rise and fall of John Z DeLorean and his doomed attempt to build the sports car of the future in 1980s war-torn Belfast. The programme features rare and unseen footage filmed by Oscar winning directors DA Pennebaker and Chris Hegedus, offering the first in-depth psychological profile of DeLorean

programme.title: Silenced: The Hidden Story of Disabled Britain
programme.desc: Cerrie Burnell presents a history of disabled people's struggle for human rights in Britain. The film looks back to the policies in place in Victorian workhouses, uncovering the original records of an institution created to segregate disabled people and stop them from having children. She also shares inspiring stories of pioneering campaigners for social change, and looks at the challenges still to be faced in the future

programme.title: Targeted: The Truth about Disability Hate Crime
programme.desc: Disabled filmmaker Richard Butchins' brutally honest film explores the abuse and aggression faced by disabled people in everyday life, from verbal insults to violent physical attacks. The film features a wide range of testimony from survivors themselves as they share their own experiences of the abuse they have faced

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham catches up on the action from the live cameras in the New Forest and on the River Ness in Scotland, while Iolo Williams follows a feral flock of barnacle geese. Elsewhere, cameras delve below the surface of Loch Carron to see the amazing brittlestars who thrive there in winter

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A distinctive sticker on a laptop finally links a man to a string of burglaries that had terrified a community, and shiny copper hair dye puts a dangerous armed robber behind bars

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Victorian Slum
programme.desc: The volunteers experience the living and working conditions of the 1890s, with cheap foodstuffs and mass-manufactured goods having found their way into the slum's shop - but only some of the residents can afford them. The Howarths' relative prosperity means Mandy can turn her attention to being a respectable Victorian house wife, while the Potters are offered a lifeline by their neighbour Maria, who needs help with her laundry business. Michael Mosley presents

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore uncovers the role of religion in creating and maintaining the power of the city. In the first episode, he explores how every event in ancient Rome revolved around worship and that nothing took place without calling on the Pantheon, the temple to their gods. He also reveals the part played by sacredness and sacrifice in Roman culture

programme.title: Ireland to Sydney by Any Means
programme.desc: The adventurer and his team make it across the Persian Gulf to Dubai, and catch a container ship to India. After soaking up the colours, smells and flavours of Mumbai, they endure a 19-hour train journey to Delhi, where they take part in a tuk-tuk race through the city. They then ride to Agra astride handmade classic motorbikes, but cameraman Mungo seriously injures his knee and is forced to return home

programme.title: West Side Stories - The Making of a Classic
programme.desc: West Side Story is one of the best-loved musicals of all time, with its timeless story and exhilarating dance and music continuing to excite audiences around the globe. Songs such as Maria, Somewhere, Tonight and America having become some of the biggest hits in showbusiness and yet the musical had an uneasy birth and was even turned away by producers when it was first put together in the 1950s. In this documentary, Strictly Come Dancing judge Bruno Tonioli and Radio 3 presenter Suzy Klein examine the origins of West Side Story, which brought together the talents of Arthur Laurents, Leonard Bernstein, Stephen Sondheim and Jerome Robbins

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: With a career spanning 50 years and a catalogue of hits to his name including Cats, Les Miserables, Phantom of the Opera and Miss Saigon, Cameron Mackintosh is about to launch hit US musical Hamilton in London. Alan Yentob meets the impresario to discover how a timber merchant's son became the most successful man in musical theatre and in the process changed the face and sound of the business across the globe

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore uncovers the role of religion in creating and maintaining the power of the city. In the first episode, he explores how every event in ancient Rome revolved around worship and that nothing took place without calling on the Pantheon, the temple to their gods. He also reveals the part played by sacredness and sacrifice in Roman culture

They then ride to Agra astride handmade classic motorbikes, but cameraman Mungo seriously injures his knee and is forced to return home

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: Fred takes his traction engine to an old friend to get it repaired, before meeting a pattern-maker who owns six similar machines and who takes him on a trip to a local foundry. Fred also visits a steam-powered museum and garden centre, and a family clock-maker, who shows him how cogs are made

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a beautiful summer scene, with a cabin and rushing rivulet flowing through a luscious green landscape

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the V&A, curator Keith is trying to breathe life into an original Stormtrooper costume from The Empire Strikes Back. However, the helmet is missing. In the Rock and Pop archive, curator Vicky is examining a photograph donated after the V&A's David Bowie Exhibition. And a road sign, made in 1961 by graphic designer Margaret Calvert, is also added to the museum's collection

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Bendor Grosvenor and Emma Dabiri visit Brighton Museum to investigate who painted two religious pictures, and uncover the story of an extravagant building

programme.title: Craftivism: Making a Difference
programme.desc: Writer, comedian and art lover Jenny Eclair meets people doing extraordinary things with knitting, cross-stitch, banners and felt to change hearts and minds across the world

programme.title: MAKE! Craft Britain
programme.desc: Martha Kearney presents this celebration of the power of craft, following ordinary people as they discover the simple pleasure of learning a new skill. It's a tale of two workshops, as Yorkshire Moors embroiderer Marna Lunt welcomes a mixed group of student to her two-day course making lampshades, while on a Sunday morning in London, six students take up their scalpels to learn the art of paper cutting with teacher Christine Green

programme.title: Handmade in Africa
programme.desc: Documentary exploring inherited knowledge and the meaning of tradition, craftsmanship and artistic processes in three African cultures, beginning with the weaving of a bamboo house in the remote highlands of southern Ethiopia by members of the Dorze minority ethnic group

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: Fred meets fellow traction engine enthusiasts in Staffordshire and visits an old friend who is restoring a triple expansion engine at Bratch Pumping Station. He also takes a tour of the trains on the Severn Valley railway and learns about the history of mining in the Black Country at the Living Museum

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints the scene from a cabin, hidden in the middle of a plush green forest and surrounded by the sounds of nature

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: In the hope of avoiding a fuss, Clegg plans a quiet birthday lunch at a nice hotel - but his pals are sure to throw a few spanners in the works. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke. Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton star

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Matter of Loaf and Death
programme.desc: The duo open their own bakery, Top Bun, and business is soon booming - not least because a 'cereal killer' is targeting all the rival bakers in town. Even better, Wallace has caught the eye of local beauty Piella Bakewell. But Gromit is more concerned that his master might become the killer's next victim. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Sally Lindsay

programme.title: Life of a Mountain: A Year on Scafell Pike
programme.desc: Documentary charting England's highest peak as it is transformed by the seasons - seen through the eyes of those who climb it for pleasure, those who try to protect its slopes, and the Cumbrian farmers who work in the surrounding valleys and fells surrounding the Lake District National Park. Contributions from the British Mountaineering Council and National Trust volunteers highlight the importance of maintaining the landscape for future generations

programme.title: The Great Mountain Sheep Gather
programme.desc: Documentary following a shepherd's journey from summit to valley as he leads his sheep off Scafell Pike, reflecting on life caring for his flock in this rugged landscape. Maxine Peake reads specially commissioned poetry written by Mark Pajak to provide a counterpoint to the shepherd's insights throughout the film.

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Josephine heads back to Paris with Youssef, but his brother Souleymane is now in grave danger, while Judge Vargas, who was in charge of Gilou's infiltration, decides to cut his losses. But what does this mean for Gilou?

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Last-minute changes on the day of the hold-up cause chaos and the police's meticulously planned intervention is thrown into disarray. programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.

Just how will he get back home?

programme.desc: Mister Maker uses his trusty Makerphone to call one of his famous friends - the incredible Jay Blades! Mister M asks Jay for help when trying to repair an old egg box. The friends decide that a Treasure Trunk needs to be made, and this proves to be an exciting and very useful idea. Mister Maker, knowing that Jay is famous for his splashes of bright colour within his designs, uses the same approach too. Later, Mister Maker checks in on his friends, The Shapes. They are snoring away, so Mister Maker makes sure he leaves them to have a nice rest. Mister Maker then decides to dance like a Shape himself, which in turn inspires him to make some shape art. This leads to another arty idea where some paper plates are put to good use. Lastly, Mister Maker remembers all the fun he has had throughout the day.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster has been chosen as the prize-picker at the Harvest Day contest, but when he gets there it turns out there is only one prize and three teams. This means he has to choose a winner between his friends - Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and the In-Chicks. Round one is fancy fashion foodies, and everyone loves Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, so Love Monster thinks he can just pick her. Then it is round two - the veggie variety show. Everyone loves the In-Chicks, so Love Monster now has to choose between them and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny. Then it is the final round, which is the tantalizing taste test of home grown goodies, and this time everyone loves Bad Idea Puppy. Love Monster has no idea how he can pick a winner and runs off and hides, but then he realises Bad Idea Puppy always makes him laugh, so he declares him the winner.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate looks at the emergency services. She uses a special camera to find out how the hose on a fire engine works and how the pump inside the fire engine creates pressure and sends water to the hose. Next, Maddie sees how an ordinary white car is made into a police car using special blue and yellow reflective stickers. A microscope camera reveals the tiny prisms inside the stickers, which make them bright and shiny

Plus, Southampton v Aston Villa, Everton v Newcastle United, Chelsea v Burnley, Crystal Palace v Wolverhampton Wanderers and West Bromwich Albion v Fulham

Jean Johansson is in the wolf wood to see if the European grey wolves can live up to their reputation for having one of the best senses of smell in the animal world

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Plant and Caroline Hawley help two teams scour an antiques fair on the island of Anglesey, while host Eric Knowles makes flour at a restored water mill in Bunbury, Cheshire

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: With three items saved from an Altrincham skip, Sarah Moore spruces up a table, Guy Trench helps an old vice find a new home, and Daniel Heath put his twist on a pair of chairs

programme.title: The NeverEnding Story (1984)
programme.desc: A lonely boy, neglected by his father and bullied at school, finds comfort in a mysterious book about a young hero's quest to save his world from destruction. However, as he reads the tale, he begins to become part of the story. Fantasy adventure based on Michael Ende's novel, starring Barret Oliver, Noah Hathaway, Gerald McRaney, Moses Gunn and Patricia Hayes

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway explores the foothills of the Cambrian Mountains and hikes around the Hafod Estate in Ceredigion. He then visits Laugharne on the Carmarthenshire coast, a favourite haunt of poet Dylan Thomas

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen explores the outer limits of the unknown, featuring tales of alien encounters, haunted houses and magical stones

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison discovers how communities on her home turf of Gloucestershire are pulling together to one another during the pandemic. She meets the producers on a mission to get everyone eating local and healthy meals at affordable prices, finds out why gardening is on prescription, and helps out with the endangered hedgehog, which is facing a precarious future. Wildlife film-maker Jack Perks gets up close to dippers, Adam Henson sizes up a new boar for his pigs and Charlotte Smith investigates what future trade deals could mean for the food on the nation's plates

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Newby Hall & Gardens in Ripon, North Yorkshire. Treasures include an intriguing letter from Lord Nelson, politely replying to his optician just weeks before the Battle of Trafalgar. There's also a piece of the set from Live Aid in 1985, rescued from a skip, and an engagement ring picked up in a pawn shop. The Bradford Museum also launches an appeal to find the families related to thousands of photos in their archive

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at the effect human activity is having, revealing how the carbon dioxide being produced is destabilising life on Earth. There is footage of rescued orphaned elephants in Kenya, victims of ever-worsening droughts, while ocean patrols off the coast of Gabon fight to save endangered sharks. In the Amazon, wildlife teams are saving animals in the shrinking rainforest, and in San Diego a zoo is freezing the DNA of endangered species before they go extinct

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Herman's behaviour becomes increasingly erratic and he is sent on compulsory leave. Will a private inspection of Kanit House bring him, Angela and their colleagues the major breakthrough they crave? Charles, meanwhile, is in Karachi attempting to set up business in Europe and as he attempts to snare a buyer for his gems, he faces up to memories of his past, while Marie-Andree and Ajay vie for ascendancy within the group

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring West Ham United v Liverpool at London Stadium, Leicester City v Leeds United at King Power Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v Tottenham Hotspur at Amex Stadium. Liverpool, Leicester and Spurs will all hold aspirations of winning the league in what promises to be a closely fought and unpredictable title-race. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show

programme.title: The Bling Ring (2013)
programme.desc: Five teenagers united by an obsession with celebrity steal from the houses of the rich and famous stars they idolise, using the internet to follow their targets' movements. When the police finally catch up with them, the arrest brings a taste of the fame they have craved. Fact-based drama, starring Emma Watson and Israel Broussard

programme.desc: After a leading trainer shot and badly wounded, a former boxer executed in broad daylight, and a gangland killing at a weigh-in for a European title fight, there is evidence that organised crime is involved in professional boxing. Darragh MacIntyre investigates the murky world behind the glamour of the big fights and the suspected gangster who has been welcomed to boxing's top table. Daniel Kinahan has been named in the courts as the head of one of Europe's biggest drug cartels. So how did he become involved in setting up the fight between Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua to become the undisputed heavyweight champion of the world?
